steps.title: Locate point H on the unit circle
steps.content:
$H = (-1, y)$ lies on the unit circle $x^2 + y^2 = 1$:
$$(-1)^2 + y^2 = 1 \Rightarrow y^2 = 0 \Rightarrow y = 0$$
So $H = (-1, 0)$, directly opposite $F = (1, 0)$ across the center $G = (0, 0)$.

steps.title: Find all possible positive measures of angle FGH
steps.content:
$F$ and $H$ are diametrically opposite, so the central angle between them is $\pi$ radians. Adding any whole number of full rotations gives the general form:
$$\angle FGH = \pi + 2\pi k = (2k+1)\pi \text{ for integer } k \ge 0$$
Valid measures are odd integer multiples of $\pi$: $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, \dots, 35\pi, \dots$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$\frac{37\pi}{2}$’: $\frac{37\pi}{2} = 18.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$. Eliminate.
  • Choice B — ‘$\frac{39\pi}{2}$’: $\frac{39\pi}{2} = 19.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — ‘$34\pi$’: $34\pi$ — an even multiple of $\pi$, which corresponds to landing back at $F$, not at $H$. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — ‘$35\pi$’: $35\pi = (2 \cdot 17 + 1)\pi$ — an odd multiple of $\pi$, valid.
That confirms D is correct.
